column1: You are carrying so much that you can hardly move.  You'd better drop something soon.
column2: 你帶著太多東西，幾乎無法移動。你最好趕快丟掉一些東西。

column1: You pick up the loose daggers.
I don't know what you're trying to drop.
You can't drop something you don't have.
column2: 你不能丟你沒有的東西。

column1: You're about as stealthy as the average Goon.
column2: 你的潛行能力就像普通的小流氓一樣。

column1: There's no point in trying that here.
You delicately insert the lockpick into your left nostril.
column2: 你輕輕地把開鎖工具插進你的左鼻孔。

column1: Unfortunately, you push it in too far, causing yourself a cerebral hemorrhage.  Guess you should have practiced some more on less difficult locks.
column2: 不幸的是，你插得太深，造成腦出血。看來你應該先在簡單的鎖上多練習。

column1: Success!  You now have an open nose.
column2: 成功！你的鼻子現在開了。

column1: You're not close enough to a door.
You have no reason to do that.

column2: 你看不到這裡有任何東西。

column1: You will not need to use the word "%s" in this game.
column2: 你在遊戲中不需要用到「%s」這個詞。

column1: Please try a different way of saying that.
I'm not sure what you're trying to do.
Every hero needs a break now and then.  Have a nice nap.
column2: 每位英雄都需要時不時休息一下。\n好好睡一覺吧。

column1: There's no need.  You're not hungry.
The rations are tasteless but filling.
You are so exhausted that everything you do hurts.  Better get some rest.
column2: 你筋疲力盡到做任何事都會傷害你。最好休息一下。

column1: You are exhausted from lack of sleep.
column2: 你因缺乏睡眠而筋疲力盡。

column1: You just ate your last ration; you'd better get some more food soon.
column2: 你剛吃掉最後一份糧食；你最好趕快找點東西吃。

column1: You're starving.  Better find some food *soon*!
column2: 你快餓死了。趕快找食物！

column1: It is the middle of the night
Quit sightseeing and watch what you're doing!
column2: 停止觀光，注意你在做什麼！

column1: You don't have time for that.
Your weapon is now magically charged.
column2: 你的武器已經被施加了魔法充能。

column1: It was a tough battle, and you lost. Never fear!  All you have to do is restore your game, and... What do you mean, "Restore WHAT game?"
column2: 這場戰鬥很激烈,你輸了。別害怕!你只需要讀檔,然後……等等,你說什麼「讀什麼檔?」

column1: You pick up a few small rocks.

column2: 你在這裡沒偵測到魔法。

column1: You don't know how to cast spells.
You don't have enough Magic Points to cast that spell.
column2: 你沒有足夠的法力來施展那個咒語。

column1: Why would you want to do that?
You don't know how to pick locks.
Oh, by the way . . .  You will need the information contained in the printed documentation to successfully complete this game.  In other words, it's not just the law . . . It's a good idea.
column2: 對了…你會需要印刷手冊裡的資訊,才能順利完成本遊戲。換句話說,這不只是法律問題…也是個好主意。

column1: Wow!  You're Really A Hero!  [score %d of 500]
column2: 哇！你真的成為英雄了！[分數 %d / 500]

column1: You still have points to allocate.
Disk file in which to save your Hero.
column2: 用來儲存英雄的磁碟檔案。

column1: You must type a description for the game.
column2: 你必須為遊戲輸入描述。

column1: Your save game disk is full. You must either use another disk or save over an existing saved game.
column2: 你的存檔磁片已滿。你必須使用另一張磁片或覆蓋現有的存檔。

column1: That game was saved under a different interpreter. It cannot be restored.
column2: 該遊戲是用不同的直譯器存檔的。無法還原。

column1: That doesn't appear to be a proper sentence.
column2: 這似乎不是適當的句子。

column1: Please insert your %s disk in drive %s.
column2: 請在 %s 磁碟機中插入你的 %s 軟碟。

column1: Sorry, there's not enough room to see that here.
column2: 抱歉，這裡沒有足夠的空間看到那個。
